title: Adding Comments 📝
description: Leave notes for yourself and others.

slides.title: Leaving Notes 📝
slides.content:
Sometimes you want to leave a note in your code for humans to read, which the computer should **ignore**.

These are called **Comments**.

quizData.question: What is a comment?
quizData.explanation: Comments are for developers, not for execution.

slides.title: Using Slashes //
slides.content:
Use **two slashes** `//` to start a comment.

`// This is a comment`

The computer will skip everything after `//` on that line.
slides.codeSnippet:
// This code does nothing
console.log('I run!'); // This runs

quizData.question: Which symbol starts a comment?
quizData.explanation: `//` is the standard single-line comment syntax in JS.

slides.title: Pro Tip 💡
slides.content:
## Disable Code

You can 'comment out' code to temporarily stop it from running without deleting it!

`// console.log('I am hidden');`
